text: The surgeon performs adhesiolysis to separate mild adhesions around the previous cystectomy scar, revealing the surgical field for further dissection.
The surgeon explores the right iliac fossa, revealing a large cystic lesion within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to remove peri-appendiceal adhesions, clearing the area around the appendix.
The surgeon identifies the base of the appendix, preparing to address it in subsequent steps of the appendectomy procedure.
The surgeon divides the meso-appendix completely, skeletonizing the entire appendix, revealing its structure and surrounding tissues.
The surgeon marks a proposed line of division on the appendix, ensuring it is at least one centimeter away from the visible cystic lesion in the right iliac fossa.
After skeletonizing the appendix, the surgeon uses a linear stapler to divide a portion of the cecum, rather than the base of the appendix.
The surgeon carefully bags the specimen and retrieves it from the surgical field, ensuring the specimen remains intact without rupture during extraction.
A small incision is made at the left iliac region to provide support for removing the specimen.
